message:
br-3jpoz: swap-extract on_exit/at_quick_exit handlers instead of holding lock

exit() and quick_exit() previously iterated their handler Vec under the
Mutex guard, so a handler that re-registered via on_exit/at_quick_exit
would self-deadlock on the non-recursive std::sync::Mutex. Sibling to
bd-sq7ae (atfork runners). Fix mirrors frankenlibc_core::stdlib::
run_atexit_handlers: take the Vec under the lock, release, iterate; loop
so newly-registered handlers run too (POSIX requires this for atexit,
and glibc's on_exit/at_quick_exit follow the same convention).

Full pthread_abi and stdlib_abi integration suites still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3hud","title":"bd-yos subtask: Join/detach lifecycle state machine and error semantics","description":"Background:
- join/detach lifecycle correctness is essential to avoid resource leaks and undefined ownership states.

Goal:
- Implement deterministic thread lifecycle state machine for join/detach/exit-value propagation.

Deliverables:
1) Thread handle lifecycle states.
2) Join semantics with exit status delivery.
3) Detach semantics and resource reclamation.
4) Error handling (double-join, join-detached, invalid handle).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Lifecycle operations are race-safe and deterministic under test.
- Error codes and edge behavior are explicit and verified.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for lifecycle transitions and misuse cases.
- Structured logs: trace_id, handle_id, op, old_state, new_state, return_value_ref, errno.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.033022201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:05.221352645Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:05.221285439Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","threading"],"comments":[{"id":997,"issue_id":"bd-3hud","author":"CrimsonForge","text":"COMPLETED: Join/detach lifecycle state machine and error semantics (bd-3hud).

Changes from bd-mjon baseline:

1. CAS-protected state transitions:
   - Trampoline: STARTING -> RUNNING via compare_exchange (prevents overwriting DETACHED)
   - Trampoline: RUNNING -> FINISHED via compare_exchange (detects DETACHED for self-cleanup)
   - join_thread: FINISHED -> JOINED via CAS (only one joiner wins)
   - detach_thread: RUNNING/STARTING -> DETACHED via CAS (only one detacher wins)
   - detach_thread: FINISHED -> DETACHED via CAS (races safely with concurrent joiner)

2. Self-join detection (EDEADLK):
   - join_thread checks if calling thread's TID matches target's TID

3. Detached thread self-cleanup:
   - Trampoline detects DETACHED state via CAS failure
   - Calls set_tid_address(NULL) to prevent kernel CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID write to freed memory
   - Frees heap-allocated ThreadHandle
   - Stack reclaimed by OS on process exit (munmap deferred to bd-rth1 for signal safety)

4. Error semantics:
   - NULL handle: EINVAL (22) for both join and detach
   - Self-join: EDEADLK (35)
   - Join already-joined: EINVAL
   - Join detached: EINVAL
   - Detach already-joined: EINVAL
   - Detach already-detached: EINVAL

5. New syscall wrapper:
   - sys_set_tid_address(tidptr) in syscall/mod.rs

Files modified:
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/pthread/thread.rs: CAS state machine, self-cleanup, error codes
- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/mod.rs: Added sys_set_tid_address

Quality gates: all PASS
Tests: 11/11 PASS (5 new for bd-3hud)","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3jh","title":"Integration: C compile/link/run fixture suite under LD_PRELOAD","description":"Critique mapping: #1.

Deliverables:
- A minimal, versioned C fixture suite under tests/integration/ that:
  - links against glibc as usual
  - is executed under LD_PRELOAD=.../libc.so
  - validates behavior (stdout + errno + safety outcomes)

Acceptance:
- Fixtures cover: malloc/free/realloc, memcpy/memmove/strlen, read/write/open/close, pthread_mutex_*.
- Results are stable and checked by harness.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): c_fixture_suite_test (7 pass) + gate script. All fixture sources exist, acceptance symbols covered, covered modules valid, fixtures have required fields.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:26.849037323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:54:16.707976Z","close_reason":"C compile/link/run fixture suite operational. 7 harness tests pass. check_c_fixture_suite.sh validates fixture sources, acceptance symbols, covered modules.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","critique","e2e"]}

Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/process_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity in strict/hardened modes, and fix any matrix-path regressions the new coverage exposes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:52:12.955090641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:36:23.152748450Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:36:23.150567471Z","close_reason":"Executed process_ops fixture coverage through the isolated harness conformance matrix path and verified via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3jpoz","title":"exit/quick_exit hold Mutex across user callbacks - self-deadlock on re-entrant on_exit/at_quick_exit","description":"stdlib_abi.rs::exit (lines 773-778) holds ON_EXIT_HANDLERS.lock() while invoking user callbacks. stdlib_abi.rs::quick_exit (lines 2355-2360) holds QUICK_EXIT_HANDLERS.lock() across user callback invocation. Any handler that transitively calls on_exit or at_quick_exit self-deadlocks on the non-recursive std::sync::Mutex. Class 1. Fix: swap-extract the Vec under the lock, drop guard, iterate extracted vec (pattern copied from frankenlibc_core::stdlib::run_atexit_handlers which already does this correctly with an outer loop for newly-registered handlers). Sibling fix to bd-sq7ae which fixed the same anti-pattern in pthread_abi.rs::run_atfork_*.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:30.975453666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:30.975453666Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3jzb","title":"BRAVO RW Locks for read-heavy paths (section 14.7)","description":"Implement BRAVO (Biased Reader And Visible Observer) reader-writer locks for read-heavy paths in FrankenLibC. BRAVO is a RW lock optimized for the common case of read-dominant workloads. Readers use a visible-reader protocol (write their TID to a shared slot on read entry, clear on exit). Writers check all slots for active readers. Key innovation: biased locking — the fast path for readers is a single atomic write (no CAS, no fence on x86-TSO). Writers pay the cost of scanning reader slots. Application to FrankenLibC: (1) Allocation metadata — the per-arena metadata (free list head, allocation count, generation counters) is read on every allocation for validation but written infrequently (only on actual alloc/free state changes). BRAVO eliminates reader contention. (2) TSM certificate cache — evaluated certificates are cached. Cache reads vastly outnumber writes (cache invalidation is rare). (3) Thread registry — the list of active threads is read by garbage collection and RCU grace period detection but modified only on thread creation/destruction. Performance target: reader fast path <5ns (single atomic store), writer path <100ns (slot scan + fence). Comparison: implement alongside RCU (bd-3aof) and Left-Right (bd-cs9w) — benchmark all three for FrankenLibC's specific read/write ratios to determine which is optimal for each use case.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","design":"**Alien CS Reference:** Section 14.7 of graveyard. BRAVO from Dice, Kogan, & Herlihy (2019 USENIX ATC). Visible-reader protocol for biased RW locks optimized for read-dominant workloads.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- BravoRwLock<T>: wraps T with visible-reader protocol.
- Reader fast path: atomic store of TID to slot in reader table. Single store on x86-TSO (no fence needed).
- Reader table: #[repr(align(64))] array of AtomicU64 slots, sized to MAX_THREADS.
- Writer: acquire inner lock (Mutex), scan reader table for active readers, wait for all to clear.
- Slot assignment: hash(thread_id) mod TABLE_SIZE for O(1) slot lookup. Collision handling: linear probe.
- read() -> BravoReadGuard: write TID to slot, read data, clear slot on drop.
- write() -> BravoWriteGuard: acquire mutex, scan table, return mutable reference.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
1. BravoRwLock<T> compiles and passes miri for basic read/write cycle.
2. Reader fast path <5ns at p99 (single atomic store on x86-TSO).
3. Writer path <100ns at p99 including slot scan under 16-reader load.
4. 32-reader + 1-writer stress test: 1M operations, TSan clean.
5. Compare with RCU and Left-Right: benchmark all three on FrankenLibC metadata workload.
6. No reader starvation: readers always make progress regardless of writer activity.
7. Slot collision rate <5% at 64 threads (hash quality test).

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"**Logging Requirements:**
- tracing::trace!(target: bravo, operation=read, slot, reader_count) per read (disabled in release).
- tracing::debug!(target: bravo, operation=write, scan_time_ns, active_readers_found) per write.
- Benchmark comparison: bravo_vs_rcu_vs_leftright.json with throughput and latency per read/write ratio.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:46.141109287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:14.456054507Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:04:14.455840361Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jzb","depends_on_id":"bd-3aof","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3kh","title":"Kernel: Proof-carrying policy tables (integrate into decide/repair selection)","description":"Wire policy table into runtime decision.

Rules:
- Strict mode: table should not change externally visible semantics; only controls validation depth.
- Hardened mode: table may choose Repair vs Deny vs FullValidate, but must respect barrier constraints.

Outputs:
- Snapshot exports policy hash and selected action distribution.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:33:33.862452216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:04:10.442423867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:50:19.509740823Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kh","depends_on_id":"bd-20s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3kh","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":998,"issue_id":"bd-3kh","author":"FuchsiaHollow","text":"Integrated policy table into decide() with policy_escalation pattern. PolicyTableLookup struct provides O(1) lookup from owned bytes. Conservative merge: table can only escalate profile, never de-escalate. Action cascade order invariant preserved by extracting Deny/Repair/FullValidate into policy_escalation variable (Allow defers to normal cascade). Snapshot exports policy_hash_prefix and action distribution. All 788 membrane tests pass, full workspace clean.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:49:58Z"},{"id":999,"issue_id":"bd-3kh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: Fixed strict-mode Deny bug - PCPT Deny/Repair clamp to FullValidate in strict mode. Added 6 integration tests. All 794 membrane tests pass. Golden snapshot and perf baseline updated.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:04:10Z"}]}

Add focused ABI regression coverage so the forbidden all-ones payload contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:37:58.401201421Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:49:57.440400208Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:49:57.438116866Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-sq7ae","title":"atfork runners hold Mutex across user callbacks — self-deadlock on re-entrant pthread_atfork","description":"run_atfork_prepare/parent/child in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:4350-4385 hold ATFORK_HANDLERS.lock() across arbitrary user-callback invocation. A prepare/parent/child handler that transitively calls pthread_atfork (common in lazy-init library paths) re-locks a non-recursive std::sync::Mutex on the same thread -> deadlock. Concrete interleaving: T1 fork() -> run_atfork_prepare() holds lock -> calls H1 -> H1 init'd some library L2 that registers its own handler via pthread_atfork -> pthread_atfork tries ATFORK_HANDLERS.lock() -> self-deadlock. Class 1 (classic mutex deadlock). Fix: snapshot handlers (Vec<AtforkHandlers> with derive(Clone, Copy)), drop guard, iterate snapshot. Affects: unistd_abi.rs:5415/5432/5434 fork interposer and process_abi.rs:139/156/159 fork interposer.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:26:44.711167663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:26:44.711167663Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-sq7ae","title":"atfork runners hold Mutex across user callbacks — self-deadlock on re-entrant pthread_atfork","description":"run_atfork_prepare/parent/child in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:4350-4385 hold ATFORK_HANDLERS.lock() across arbitrary user-callback invocation. A prepare/parent/child handler that transitively calls pthread_atfork (common in lazy-init library paths) re-locks a non-recursive std::sync::Mutex on the same thread -> deadlock. Concrete interleaving: T1 fork() -> run_atfork_prepare() holds lock -> calls H1 -> H1 init'd some library L2 that registers its own handler via pthread_atfork -> pthread_atfork tries ATFORK_HANDLERS.lock() -> self-deadlock. Class 1 (classic mutex deadlock). Fix: snapshot handlers (Vec<AtforkHandlers> with derive(Clone, Copy)), drop guard, iterate snapshot. Affects: unistd_abi.rs:5415/5432/5434 fork interposer and process_abi.rs:139/156/159 fork interposer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:26:44.711167663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: snapshot ATFORK_HANDLERS under lock, iterate snapshot unlocked. Regression test added in tests/pthread_abi_test.rs. Landed in 437e3d0d.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-sttk9","title":"fuzz: read/write/pread/pwrite buffer + count validation","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_read_write.rs targeting frankenlibc_abi::unistd_abi::{read,write,pread,pwrite,readv,writev}. Focus on buffer-bound validation + short-read/short-write semantics + errno correctness for invalid fd/len/buf.

## Archetype
1 + 3: Crash-detector + **differential** against libc for matched calls, using a seeded pipe pair and /dev/zero / /dev/null fds as deterministic backends.

## Inputs
struct ReadWriteFuzzInput {
  op: u8,                 // 0=read, 1=write, 2=pread, 3=pwrite, 4=readv, 5=writev
  fd_selector: u8,        // 0=/dev/zero, 1=/dev/null, 2=pipe-read-end, 3=pipe-write-end, 4=closed-fd, 5=-1
  buf_len: u16,           // 0..=32768
  buf_offset: u8,         // shift within a larger allocation (exercises decide's address checks)
  poison_ptr: bool,       // NULL or address we can catch
  offset: i64,            // for pread/pwrite
  iovec_count: u8,        // 0..=8 for readv/writev
}

## Invariants
- No panic on any input
- rc is a non-negative byte count OR -1 with errno in {EBADF, EFAULT, EINVAL, EIO, EAGAIN, EINTR, EPIPE, ENOSPC, ESPIPE, EOVERFLOW}
- Host parity for the /dev/zero, /dev/null, closed-fd cases: ours matches libc::read/write bit-for-bit
- readv/writev total byte count equals the sum of per-iovec byte counts
- Buffer is not read/written past requested len (ASAN + explicit sentinel check before and after)

## Expected bug classes
- Off-by-one in iovec loop, iov_len overflow, fd validation bypass, membrane decision mismatches for cross-page buffers, missing EINTR retry, sign handling for large counts (SSIZE_MAX), ESPIPE not mapped for pread/pwrite on pipes

## Sentinel pattern
Allocate 2*buf_len + 2 pages, place guard bytes (0xAA) before and after the requested window; after the call assert guard bytes unchanged.

## Score
5 × 5 × /2 ≈ 12.5 — tied with bd-o4yda for priority.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:39.154205881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:22.578845312Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:22.576538966Z","close_reason":"fuzz_read_write harness committed and green: 16.3M execs / 108K exec/s in 151s. Covers read, write, pread, pwrite, readv, writev against 5 deterministic fd backends (/dev/zero, /dev/null, pipe read, pipe write, closed fd, wild fd). Sentinel-byte guards before/after every buffer window catch any over-count write. Differential parity against libc for non-pipe backends. Two harness-side bugs fixed during bring-up (count > buf_size overrun, buf_offset into post-pad). Zero real impl bugs under the invariant set — the read/write wrapper is solid. Committed 4e263808.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sttk9","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:44.052743346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-sx5d","title":"syscall: timerfd expiration-count veneer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:09:20.834401162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T14:14:28.013948698Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T14:14:28.012034219Z","close_reason":"Implemented timerfd expiration-count veneer","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
